s-1.ptxt: DISCONNECT CABLE FROM NEGATIVE BATTERY TERMINAL
REMOVE FRONT WIPER ARM AND BLADE ASSEMBLY LH

s2.ptxt: Remove the nut and the front wiper arm and blade assembly LH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT WIPER ARM AND BLADE ASSEMBLY RH

s2.ptxt: Remove the nut and the front wiper arm and blade assembly RH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT FENDER TO COWL SIDE SEAL LH

s2.ptxt: Disengage the claw and remove the front fender to cowl side seal LH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT FENDER TO COWL SIDE SEAL RH

s2.ptxt: Disengage the claw and remove the front fender to cowl side seal RH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE COWL TOP VENTILATOR LOUVER SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 clips.
Disengage the 4 claws and remove the cowl top ventilator louver sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE INNER REAR VIEW MIRROR ASSEMBLY (for TMC Made without EC Mirror)

s2.ptxt: Disengage the 2 claws and the cover.
Pull the lever in the direction indicated by the arrow shown in the illustration and slide the inner rear view mirror assembly upward to remove it.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE INNER REAR VIEW MIRROR ASSEMBLY (for TMMK Made without EC Mirror)

s2.ptxt: Using a "torx" socket wrench (T20), remove the screw.
Slide the inner rear view mirror assembly in the direction indicated by the arrow shown in the illustration to remove it.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE INNER REAR VIEW MIRROR STAY HOLDER COVER (w/ EC Mirror)

s2.ptxt: Disengage the 2 claws and slide the inner rear view mirror stay holder cover as shown in the illustration.
Disengage the 6 claws and remove the inner rear view mirror stay holder cover.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE INNER REAR VIEW MIRROR ASSEMBLY (w/ EC Mirror)

s2.ptxt: Disconnect the connector.
Using a "torx" socket wrench (T20), remove the screw and the inner rear view mirror.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT DOOR OPENING TRIM WEATHERSTRIP LH
REMOVE FRONT DOOR OPENING TRIM WEATHERSTRIP RH
REMOVE FRONT PILLAR GARNISH LH

s2.ptxt: Pull the upper part of the garnish toward the inside of the cabin and disengage the 2 clips.
Cut off the clip <A>.
Disengage the 2 claws and remove the front pillar garnish LH.
Remove the clip <A> from the vehicle body.
Protect the curtain shield airbag assembly.

s3.ptxt: Cover the airbag with a 700 mm (27.56 in.) x 120 mm (4.72 in.) cloth or piece of nylon and fix the ends of the cover with tape, as shown in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: Cover the curtain shield airbag with a protective cover as soon as the front pillar garnish is removed.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT PILLAR GARNISH RH

s2.ptxt: Pull the upper part of the garnish toward the inside of the cabin and disengage the 2 clips.
Cut off the clip <A>.
Disengage the 2 claws and remove the front pillar garnish RH.
Remove the clip <A> from the vehicle body.
Protect the curtain shield airbag assembly.

s3.ptxt: Cover the airbag with a 700 mm (27.56 in.) x 120 mm (4.72 in.) cloth or piece of nylon and fix the ends of the cover with tape, as shown in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: Cover the curtain shield airbag with a protective cover as soon as the front pillar garnish is removed.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE ROOF CONSOLE BOX ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Using a moulding remover, disengage the 2 clips and 2 claws.
Disconnect the connectors and remove the roof console box assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE VISOR ASSEMBLY LH

s2.ptxt: Using a moulding remover, disengage the 4 claws and remove the visor bracket cover.
Disengage the 2 clips and remove the visor assembly LH.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE VISOR ASSEMBLY RH

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedures for the RH side and the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE VISOR HOLDER

s2.ptxt: Turn the visor holder approximately 45° and pull it out as shown in the illustration.
Disengage the 2 claws and remove the visor holder.

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedure for the RH side and the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FRONT ASSIST GRIP SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, disengage the 6 claws and remove the 2 assist grip covers.

atten4.ptxt: Tape the screwdriver tip before use.

s2.ptxt: Disengage the 2 clips and remove the front assist grip sub-assembly.

atten4.ptxt: Use the same procedure for the RH side and the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE ROOF HEADLINING ASSEMBLY (w/o Sliding Roof)

content1.ptxt: It is not necessary to completely remove the roof headlining assembly. Slightly lower the front section of the roof headlining assembly so that the windshield glass can be removed (See page ).

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE ROOF HEADLINING ASSEMBLY (w/ Sliding Roof)

content1.ptxt: It is not necessary to completely remove the roof headlining assembly. Slightly lower the front section of the roof headlining assembly so that the windshield glass can be removed (See page ).

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE WINDSHIELD MOULDING

s2.ptxt: Using a knife, cut off the moulding, as shown in the illustration.

item.ptxt: Do not damage the vehicle body with the knife.
If the vehicle body is damaged, repair the damaged portion by applying an anti-rust coating after removing the windshield glass.

s2.ptxt: Remove the remaining moulding from the windshield glass.

atten4.ptxt: When removing the moulding, make a partial cut, then pull and remove it by hand.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE WINDSHIELD GLASS ASSEMBLY

item.ptxt: There are No. 1 and No. 2 stoppers on the windshield glass as shown in the illustration. Be careful not to damage the windshield glass when cutting off the adhesive.
To prevent the windshield glass from dropping when performing this operation, be sure to hold the windshield glass using suction cups.

atten4.ptxt: Depending on vehicles, a 1-piece or 2-piece type stopper is used.

s2.ptxt: Apply protective tape to the installation position of the windshield glass assembly on the vehicle body.

atten4.ptxt: Apply protective tape to the installation surface to prevent it from being scratched.

s2.ptxt: Pass a piano wire between the vehicle body and glass from the interior.
Tie both wire ends to wooden blocks or similar objects.
Cut off the adhesive by pulling the piano wire around the windshield glass.

item.ptxt: When separating the windshield glass, take care not to damage the paint or the interior and exterior ornaments.
To prevent the safety pad from being scratched when removing the windshield glass, place a plastic sheet between the piano wire and safety pad.

s2.ptxt: Place matchmarks on the windshield glass and vehicle body on the locations indicated in the illustration.

atten4.ptxt: Matchmarks do not need to be placed if the windshield glass is not going to be reused.

s2.ptxt: Using a suction cup, remove the windshield glass assembly.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to drop the windshield glass.
Leave as much adhesive on the vehicle body as possible when removing the windshield glass.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE WINDOW GLASS ADHESIVE DAM

s2.ptxt: Using a scraper, remove the adhesive dam.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the windshield glass.
Be sure to replace the adhesive dam with a new one.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 2 WINDSHIELD GLASS STOPPER

s2.ptxt: Using a scraper, remove the 2 No. 2 stoppers.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the windshield glass.
Be sure to replace the No. 2 stoppers with new ones.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE WINDSHIELD GLASS RETAINER

s2.ptxt: Using a scraper, remove the 2 retainers.

item.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the windshield glass.
Be sure to replace the retainer with a new one.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 1 WINDSHIELD GLASS STOPPER (for 2-piece Type)

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 No. 1 stoppers.

atten3.ptxt: Be sure to replace the No. 1 stoppers with new ones.

s-1.ptxt: CLEAN WINDSHIELD GLASS

s2.ptxt: Using a scraper, remove the adhesive tape and adhesive sticking to the windshield glass.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the windshield glass.

s2.ptxt: Clean the outer circumference of the windshield glass with a non-residue solvent.

item.ptxt: Do not touch the windshield glass surface after cleaning it.
Even if using a new windshield glass, clean the windshield glass with a non-residue solvent.

s-1.ptxt: CLEAN VEHICLE BODY

s2.ptxt: Clean and shape the contact surface of the vehicle body.

s3.ptxt: Using a knife, cut away any rough adhesive on the contact surface of the vehicle body to ensure the appropriate surface shape.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the vehicle body.

atten4.ptxt: Leave as much adhesive on the vehicle body as possible.

s3.ptxt: Clean the contact surface of the vehicle body with a piece of cloth saturated with cleaner.

atten4.ptxt: Even if all the adhesive has been removed, clean the vehicle body.
